channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DNI Gabbard sounds off on Europe's 'divergence' from U.S. values after tense Trump-Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump's meeting with Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy exposed some European nations' “divergence" from promoting freedom, DNI Tulsi Gabbard said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: Demonstrators have gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. to protest the automaker’s billionaire leader, Elon Musk. Protesters are targeting Musk over his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: When President Donald Trump speaks to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday, his words are bound to be far different from when he addressed lawmakers at the beginning of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Calvin Woodward, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge rules head of watchdog agency must keep his job, says Trump's bid to oust him was unlawful
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that the head of a federal watchdog agency must remain in his job, saying President Donald Trump’s bid to remove the special counsel was unlawful.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump reports illegal immigrant encounters at historic lows during first full month in office
item.description: The Trump administration announced Saturday that Customs and Border Patrol apprehensions of illegal immigrants reached a record-setting low in the president's first full month in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the US-Mexico border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the U.S.-Mexico border as President Donald Trump seeks to clamp down on illegal immigration and fulfill a central promise of his campaign, U.S. officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo launches NYC mayoral bid
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo launched his bid for New York City mayor with a video posted on his X account. This comes after much speculation that the governor would launch a comeback after leaving office in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Zelenskyy moves on after Oval Office blowout with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has thanked the American people and leadership and voiced hope for “strong relations,” a day after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with U.S. President Donald Trump that left many uncertain where the once staunch allies stood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Frustrations mount as Stefanik stuck in limbo
item.description: Frustrations are growing as Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) wait to be confirmed as ambassador to the United Nations could stretch until April due to the House’s ultra-slim majority. Stefanik is facing a unique problem. Despite numerous international crises facing the U.S. and U.N.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees are starting to receive another email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Sen. Shannon Grove introduces bill for additional protection for sex trafficking victims, fines for perpetrators
item.description: Grove's previous proposals -- which were signed into law -- exclude cases with 16 and 17-year-old victims.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's top prosecutor in DC demotes several supervisors who handled politically sensitive cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Several senior leaders in Washington's federal prosecutors office have been demoted to jobs handling misdemeanors or other low-level matters, in the latest move by President Donald Trump's U.S. attorney to roil the office that oversaw the massive prosecution of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and BYRON TAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musicians, bus driver caught with 200 pounds of cocaine
item.description: Officers sent the bus to secondary inspection after attempting to cross the Hidalgo-Reynosa International Bridge, where the discovery was made.
item.dc:creator: Dave Hendricks, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans seek major changes to House-passed Trump budget bill
item.description: Senate Republicans are staring down a major fight to overhaul the House's budget resolution as lawmakers eye big changes. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.), with the help of President Trump’s muscle, was able to get the House's plan to enact Trump's sweeping legislative agenda past a key hurdle Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: DFL, GOP team up on bills to battle absenteeism in Minnesota schools
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers this week are moving forward on five bills aimed at addressing chronic absenteeism in Minnesota schools, and DFLers and Republicans are working together on policy solutions.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Federal freeze on energy and climate funding causing concern, confusion in Minnesota
item.description: Some of the money has been released since federal judges ordered the funding to be unfrozen. But weatherization programs and rebates for home efficiency upgrades are still on hold. State and local officials say a lack of communication has led to confusion and uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump shifts date for Canada and Mexico tariffs
item.description: Little about how Trump discusses tariffs is normal — not only because he threatens tariffs on a weekly, even daily, basis, but also because it's often unclear if or when those tariffs will happen.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: NIH partially lifts freeze on funding process for medical research
item.description: Thousands of grant applications had been stalled when the Trump administration blocked the National Institutes of Health from posting notices to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: National parks already feel the effects of layoffs, even before the busy season starts
item.description: Some 1,000 NPS employees were fired, and hiring for seasonal positions was delayed. Here’s what to know about the impacts already being felt at parks, and what it could mean for the busy season.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s social media video garners pushback from Arabs and Muslims in U.S. and Gaza
item.description: In a seemingly AI-generated video that the president posted on social media, images of destruction due to the war in Gaza are transformed into a glitzy resort called “TRUMP GAZA.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, Hadeel Al-Shalchi, and Anas Baba, MPR News

item.title: Public records disputes target of Nevada lawmaker's bill creating an ombudsman
item.description: A tug-of-war lies ahead as groups look to influence legislation designed to resolve disputes over access to public records.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Democrat proposes study on slavery's impact, possible reparations
item.description: A Nevada Democratic assemblymember is proposing a bill to create a commission to study discrimination against Black Americans and possible reparations.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Smog checks every 2 years good enough under new proposal at Nevada Legislature
item.description: Smog checks would be required once every two years instead of annually under legislation sponsored by a Democrat and a Republican in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
